articles.title: Baby Dear
articles.author: Abbie R. Madsen
articles.complete_text:
Baby Dear
By Abbie R. Madsen

Shut your weary, tired eyes,
Baby dear, Baby dear.
Let me hold you ere time flies,
Baby dear!
Twilight shadows softly creep,
You should be in slumber deep,
Heaven guard you while you sleep,
Baby dear.

Soon these little feet will go,
Baby dear, Baby dear.
In many paths I do not know,
Baby dear!
Just this one thought comes to me:
When you leave us, will you be
Not too proud to bend the knee,
Baby dear?

I would be with you all day,
Baby dear, Baby dear.
But your path will lead away,
Baby dear.
You will meet conditions new,
Heaven grant you may prove true;
That is all I'd ask for you,
Baby dear.
articles.notes: This is a complete poem by Abbie R. Madsen that appears in the uncovered text section. It's a three-stanza poem about a baby/child, with clear poetic structure and religious themes typical of Relief Society Magazine content.

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of advertisements for LDS garments, bread, phonographs, and various Salt Lake City businesses; table of contents for February 1921 issue; General Board member listings; and advertisers' directory - all legitimate miscellaneous content.
